Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Quash For Improper Service Of Process And Lack Of Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Dec-10-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Zachary Alinder, Esq. (415.392.1960; zalinder@sideman.com) of Sideman & Bancroft LLP for Specially Appearing Defendants.
No other appearances.

DEFENDANTS PATRICK STRATEMAN and INTERSANGO LLC'S Motion To Quash For Improper Service Of Process And Lack Of Jurisdiction.

"Specially appearing defendants Patrick Strateman and Intersango LLC's motion to quash for improper service of process" is granted. Jurisdiction can be addressed if and when defendants are properly served.

First, plaintiff does not show "reasonable diligence" to try to effect personal service. (CCP 415.20(b).) Second, the statute requires service at "the person's dwelling house, usual place of abode, usual place of business, or usual mailing address" (id.), but plaintiff shows none of these. Plaintiff does not indicate why he claims a San Francisco house is defendant Patrick Stratemore's "last known address." (Stratemore says he lives in Canada.) Moreover, notes from a process server report that "mother/occupant" stated Stratemore "does not live here." The most plaintiff asserts is hearsay that "this is a legal address for him," but that term does not appear in CCP 415.20.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
